title: Finding
description: A security finding produced by the policy engine and risk engine after an exploit chain execution. Stored as JSONB in redteam_findings.details. Identity and promoted fields (id, test_id, severity, title, exploit_chain_id, policy_violation_type, primary_compliance_ref, evidence_trace_hash) are Postgres columns on redteam_findings and are NOT repeated here.

compliance_refs.description: Zero or more compliance framework mappings for this finding. v1: populated by ComplianceMapper with owasp_llm_top10 refs. v2+: additional frameworks (nist_ai_rmf, mitre_atlas, eu_ai_act, etc.) are optional steps in the test pipeline.

remediations.description: One or more targeted fix suggestions. Multiple items are generated when a finding spans node types or exploit techniques — e.g. one fix for the database node, another for the tool calling it.

log_correlation.description: Correlated application log data (v1.5 feature). Null in v1.

attack_details.description: Structured detail about the attack that produced this finding.

exploit_path.description: Ordered list of node IDs traversed during the exploit chain.

exploitability.description: Probability that this exploit would succeed against the live system (0–1).

blast_radius.description: Fraction of system affected if exploit succeeds (0–1).
